input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10. This is a known change in .NET MAUI — let me provide guidance based on what I know.

In .NET MAUI for .NET 9 and earlier, on Android you could use `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` to make the layout resize when the keyboard appears. However, this API was deprecated in Android 30+ and was removed/changed in .NET 10.

The modern approach for .NET 10 (and Android 11+) is to use the `WindowInsets` API / edge-to-edge display model. In .NET MAUI .NET 10, the recommended approach is to use the `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` behavior or handle insets properly.


The key issue is that .NET MAUI .NET 10 moved to edge-to-edge rendering by default on Android, which breaks the old `AdjustResize` behavior. The solution is to leverage `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` or manually handle the window insets using the newer `WindowInsetsCompat` API instead. when the keyboard appears, though for a chat interface with a fixed input field at the bottom, I need something more robust. The modern approach in .NET MAUI .NET 10 is to use platform-specific listeners that detect IME insets and adjust the layout padding dynamically, rather than relying on the deprecated `adjustResize` mode.
